summary: Gruber reflects on Apple's September 2022 'Far Out' event, covering the format shift to pre-filmed presentations, the iPhone 14, Apple Watch Ultra and SE, and AirPods Pro. He argues the pre-filmed format is a net win, allowing faster pacing, more presenters, and tighter production. He praises AirPods Pro as the purest expression of Apple's values today — simple, useful, no feature creep, no subscription. He finds the Apple Watch Ultra surprisingly well-priced at $799 and calls the Dynamic Island the most exciting UI concept since iPhone X.
key_points: The pre-filmed keynote format is a net positive: faster pacing, more presenters, and cinematic transitions, though some live-presentation drama is lost.
AirPods Pro are Apple's best single product expression — simple, useful, well-designed, no feature creep, no subscription, nothing left to complain about.
Apple's emphasis on safety features (crash detection, satellite SOS) is strategically smart, drawing a Volvo analogy where marketing safety sells products.
The Apple Watch Ultra at $799 is surprisingly affordable, only $50 more than stainless steel Series 8, and its action button is so useful it should come to all models.
The Dynamic Island is the most exciting new UI concept from Apple since the iPhone X's reimagined experience.
money_quotes: The new AirPods Pro are the best single expression of Apple as a company today.
What I like best about AirPods Pro, and why I say they're the single best expression of Apple today, is that there's nothing left to complain about them.
They set out to do few things but they do all of those few things well, and in ways that typical users can discover and use.
You buy them, you set them up, you use them. They integrate very well with all of Apple's other products. They really just disappear into your daily life.
Interesting things happen when interesting people are in the same place. Interesting things don't happen over Webex group meetings.
The Dynamic Island is the most exciting new UI concept from Apple since the iPhone X's reimagined iPhone experience.
As Dizzy Dean said, 'It ain't bragging if you can back it up.'
With this format, entirely pre-recorded, we'll never see an Apple feature demo fail again. That drama energizes a live presentation. Something has been lost.
key_insight: AirPods Pro represent Apple at its best — doing few things but doing all of them exceptionally well, with no feature creep, no subscriptions, and nothing left to complain about.
